results.title: PgDog Competitors & Alternatives (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:

SocialX

# PgDogAlternatives

Looking beyond PgDog? Try Supabase for an open-source backend with Postgres built in. Go serverless with Neon and branching, or choose PlanetScale for Vitess-powered MySQL at global scale. Prefer a type-safe data layer? Prisma shines. For reactive, real-time apps, explore Convex.

## Alternatives Discussion

results.content:
logo

# Comparison to other poolers¶

PgDog aims to be the de facto PostgreSQL proxy and pooler. Below is a feature comparison between PgDog and a few popular alternatives.

## Basics¶

I've been thinking about it as well. I wrote some code to prototype it here:  | | | |  |  | imbradn on May 28, 2025  | root | parent | next (javascript:void(0))   We used it to shard a database at Instacart after your time instead of the “replicate everything and drop the data on the shard approach”. That combined with dropping all the indexes (minus the primary key) worked well for the data copy/initial sharding. | | | |  |  | levkk on May 28, 2025  | root | parent | next (javascript:void(0))   That's good to know. We just need ergonomic wrappers at the DB layer to make them easy to use. | | | |  |  | grncdr on May 27, 2025  | root | parent | next (javascript:void(0))   It’s an interesting idea, but how would such a system handle queries that should cross tenant boundaries? (E.g. system-level reporting) | | | |  |  | mdaniel on May 27, 2025  | root | parent | next (javascript:void(0))   1. Go around pgcat/pgdog? 2. I have had good luck using pragma comments for that kind of thing: a way to communicate to the infrastructure without the target system seeing [...] | |  |  | Show HN: PgDog – Shard Postgres without extensions (github.com/pgdogdev) | | 307 points by levkk on May 26, 2025 | hide | past | favorite | 80 comments | | Hey HN! Lev here, author of PgDog (). I’m scaling our favorite database, PostgreSQL. PgDog is a new open source proxy, written in Rust, with first-class support for sharding — without changes to your app or needing database extensions. Here’s a walkthrough of how it works:   Running Postgres at scale is hard. Eventually, one primary isn’t enough at which point you need to split it up. Since there is currently no good tooling out there to do this, teams end up breaking their apps apart instead.  If you’re familiar with PgCat, my previous project, PgDog is its spiritual successor but with a fresh codebase and new goals. If not,

results.title: PostgreSQL Enterprise Manager Alternatives - DNSstuff
results.content:
1. ### Datadog PostgreSQL Performance Monitoring

datadog sqlserver dashboard

© Datadog 2020. All rights reserved.

Datadog is a Software as a Service (SaaS) platform with a range of system monitoring tools. The Datadog APM module features a specialist integration designed for monitoring Postgres databases. This SQL manager for PostgreSQL tracks the operating throughput of the RDBMS and proactively identifies inefficient queries consuming resources.

Datadog Postgres monitoring dashboard is packed with data visualizations, which can be slightly overwhelming. You can manage this flood of information by establishing thresholds and alerts. When a threshold is breached, you’ll receive a notification, eliminating the need to keep a constant eye on the dashboard metrics. [...] The Datadog dashboard is customizable, allowing you to reorganize information to reflect your priorities. You can also create stack views covering the services supporting your Postgres database, in addition to the RDBMS and query performance. If you want to try the Datadog unlimited monitoring package, you can download a 14-day free trial here.

1. ### REPMGR

repmgr

© 2011-2020, 2ndQuadrant Limited. All rights reserved.

REPMGR is an open-source Postgres failover manager, PostgreSQL cluster manager, and PostgreSQL replication manager. PostgreSQL’s built-in hot-standby capabilities are enhanced by REPMGR, which can set up standby servers, monitor replication, and perform administrative tasks such as failover or manual switchover operations.
